hero_summary: Rast is one of the most important core maqamat in Arabic music. It is not understood only from its scale, but from its modal movement, its points of repose, and the coloring that appears especially in the upper register.
short_identity: A reference maqam built on a firm tonic, a clearly audible Sikah third, a strong expansion toward the fifth, and a decisive return to the tonic.

quick_facts.upper_jins: Usually Rast on Nawa, with the jawab often extending to Kardan
quick_facts.character: Foundational, stable, balanced, and direct in phrase shape with clear room for modal coloring
quick_facts.common_start: It usually begins by stabilizing the lower Rast jins and making the Sikah color clear before any upper coloring appears

sayr.summary: Rast is one of the central reference maqamat in Arabic music. It is not understood only from its scalar form, but from its way of moving, its points of repose, and the coloring that becomes especially important in the upper region.
resting_tones: Rast (C): the tonic and main center of gravity
Kardan (upper C): an important upper resting point
Dukah (D) and Sikah (E half-flat): two important tones in shaping the maqam’s personality
motion_notes: The identity of Rast appears especially through the Sikah third (E half-flat)
The fifth is one of the maqam’s most important working regions
Hijaz on the fifth may function as an internal coloring inside Rast rather than a full departure from it
common_path: Bring out the Sikah color clearly

common_modulations: Not every upper coloring means the maqam has left Rast; the crucial point is the logic of phrase and repose
